# Concepts

## Experience lanes

Chat, Zen, Coffee, Sandbox, and Story are distinct lanes with different emotional weight and control surfaces.

Chat is the continuity companion lane, Zen is calmer 1:1 interaction, Coffee is multi-bot conversation, Sandbox is the runtime-control lane, and Story is the emerging playable narrative lane. The split protects both practical control and experiential clarity.

Files: `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `docs/applets.md`

Tags: `audience:all`, `ux`, `features`, `homepage`

## Free desktop distribution

Official Prism desktop builds are free; optional Patreon support is patronage, not product access.

The active distribution model is direct desktop releases for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus an iPhone PWA path served by Prism. Support should remain quiet, optional, non-gating, and separate from core functionality.

Files: `README.md`, `docs/distribution-model.md`, `docs/product-worthy-launch.md`

Tags: `audience:user`, `distribution`, `support`, `getting-started`

Record: `concept_98e450e7-a536-4429-9c00-75d47211ac3e` | Authority: agent | Confidence: high

## LOCAL mode privacy boundary

The LOCAL/ONLINE toggle is a hard privacy boundary: LOCAL turns must not silently leave the user's machine.

Provider selection, auxiliary model work, embeddings, and image generation all need to preserve the visible LOCAL promise. External calls are allowed only through explicit online/provider paths or configured local-network services such as Ollama and Qdrant.

Files: `AGENTS.md`, `apps/api/src/providers.ts`, `apps/api/src/__tests__/providers.test.ts`

Tags: `audience:all`, `privacy`, `local-first`, `architecture`, `homepage`

## Memory and continuity pipeline

Prism stores scoped conversation state, encrypted memory records, and summary/search context while respecting incognito boundaries.

The backend keeps users, sessions, conversations, messages, bots, memories, memory summaries, images, and exports scoped by user. Incognito conversations avoid memory reads and writes. Semantic memory uses local embedding paths rather than hidden cloud fallback.

Files: `AGENTS.md`, `apps/api/src/db.ts`, `apps/api/src/memory.ts`, `apps/api/src/memory-summarizer.ts`

Tags: `audience:developer`, `architecture`, `data-model`

Record: `concept_c2d00b3b-6b79-4faf-ac21-b3b4e7e022e6` | Authority: agent | Confidence: medium

## Monorepo architecture

Prism is a Node.js, Next.js, Electron, SQLite, Qdrant, and Ollama workspace organized as apps plus shared packages.

The API owns local data, auth, providers, memory, and orchestration. The web app owns the product interface. The desktop app wraps and stages the runtime. Shared packages hold cross-surface contracts and model-routing helpers.

Files: `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `apps/api`, `apps/web`, `apps/desktop`, `packages/shared`, `packages/config`

Tags: `audience:developer`, `architecture`, `dx`

Record: `concept_17097483-9143-4f39-91de-8ad33a73b888` | Authority: agent | Confidence: high

## Product promise

Prism is a private-by-default, creative-by-design AI workspace for calm, multi-perspective thinking.

Prism gives people one controlled place to think, create, and experiment with AI while keeping routine and sensitive work local. Cloud models are available when they earn their place, but provider choice and behavior remain visible to the user.
